risk_title: Political and Governance Fragility
risk_summary: Political and governance risks are rated as High at project approval, indicating significant fragility in the operating environment that could affect project implementation and stakeholder engagement.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance High High

risk_title: Governance Capacity Constraints in Implementation
risk_summary: The Project Implementation Team within the Ministry of Fisheries and Maritime Economy is newly established and working toward project implementation in a context of high political and governance risk, which may constrain effective execution and coordination.
evidence_quote: The Project Implementation Team within the Ministry of Fisheries and Maritime Economy is working toward project implementation.

risk_title: Political and Governance Risk Remains High
risk_summary: Political and governance risks are rated as High across all assessment periods (approval, last approved rating, and proposed rating), indicating persistent governance fragility affecting project implementation in Guinea.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance High High High

risk_title: Contractor Non-Performance and Potential Insecurity in Mambia Region
risk_summary: Complete lack of progress on 322 km of road rehabilitation in Mambia with contract termination planned, suggesting possible security concerns or governance breakdown preventing contractor operations in the region.
evidence_quote: In Mambia, no progress has been made on 106 km of roads; contracts will be terminated and new contractors recruited.

risk_title: Institutional Capacity and Partner Coordination Challenges
risk_summary: Persistent communication challenges between PIU and partners, combined with technical support firm demobilizing staff without replacement plan, indicate institutional fragility affecting project coordination and implementation continuity.
evidence_quote: PIU collaboration has improved, but partner communication remains a challenge. The technical support firm is demobilizing staff without a replacement plan, which must be clarified to ensure service continuity.

risk_title: Institutional Capacity for Implementation Downgraded
risk_summary: Institutional capacity rating declined from Substantial at approval to Moderate in current assessment, indicating weakening government and implementing agency capacity to sustain project implementation.
evidence_quote: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability Substantial Moderate Moderate

risk_title: Political and Governance Fragility
risk_summary: The project faces a High-rated Political and Governance risk that is affecting implementation. This reflects underlying political instability and governance challenges in Guinea that constrain project execution and institutional reforms.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance High High

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Constraints Linked to Governance Fragility
risk_summary: Substantial institutional capacity risks are present for implementation and sustainability, likely compounded by governance fragility. This affects the ability of water and sanitation institutions to execute project activities and sustain reforms.
evidence_quote: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability Substantial Substantial

risk_title: Environment and Social Tensions
risk_summary: High-rated Environment and Social risks are affecting the project, potentially including tensions with affected communities, displacement concerns, or social instability related to water infrastructure development in Greater Conakry.
evidence_quote: Environment and Social High High

risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: The project faces persistent substantial political and governance risks that constrain implementation progress. The document indicates that while key activities are underway, implementation acceleration is needed, suggesting governance-related delays in advancing reforms and institutional capacity building.
evidence_quote: While key activities are underway, project implementation should be accelerated to ensure the sustainability of project achievements.

risk_title: Institutional Capacity and Governance Constraints in Mining Sector Reforms
risk_summary: Multiple critical mining sector regulatory reforms remain incomplete or delayed, including Protected Area Code, Water Code, and mining permits exclusion from classified forests. These delays reflect governance fragility and institutional capacity limitations affecting the project's core objectives.
evidence_quote: Mining permits excluded from classified forests (Yes/No) Comments on achieving targets Classified forests outlined and excluded from licensing possibilities in the mining cadastre A directory of protected areas was produced in 2019, and the revision of the protected areas network began at the end of 2022. However, this data is not currently included in the mining cadastre.

risk_title: Governance Tensions Between Mining and Environmental Protection Mandates
risk_summary: Coordination challenges between the Ministry of Mining and environmental authorities are evident in the delayed integration of protected areas data into the mining cadastre. A dialogue with the Ministry of Mining is only scheduled for the second half of 2025, indicating governance friction.
evidence_quote: With the support of AT OGPRNRF, work to identify overlaps has been underway since November 2024. As part of the protected area review process, a dialogue with the Ministry of Mining is scheduled for second half of 2025.

risk_title: Weak Institutional Capacity for Environmental and Social Monitoring
risk_summary: The project's environmental and social monitoring capacity remains underdeveloped, with the modernization of the environmental and social assessment database still not achieved as of June 2025. This reflects institutional fragility in governance structures.
evidence_quote: b. Modernization and generalization of the database of the environmental and social assessment database to all relevant projects (Yes/No) Comments on achieving targets Capacity of AGEE in identifying and mapping all ongoing or planned infrastructure projects (dams, roads, railways, mines, etc.) to be environmentally and socially monitored June 2025: The TA for AGEE started with implementation support.

risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: The project operates in a context of high political and governance risk, which is rated as 'High' across all assessment periods. This persistent high rating indicates ongoing political instability and governance fragility that could undermine project implementation and beneficiary access to social protection services.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance High High High

risk_title: Elevated Fiduciary Risk Emerging During Implementation
risk_summary: Fiduciary risk has escalated from 'Substantial' to 'High' in the most recent assessment, suggesting emerging concerns related to financial management, procurement, or governance controls that may be linked to the fragile political context and could compromise project fund utilization.
evidence_quote: Fiduciary Substantial Substantial High

risk_title: Infrastructure Destruction from Fuel Depot Explosion
risk_summary: December 2023 fuel depot explosion in Conakry destroyed schools and administrative buildings, necessitating emergency response and CERC activation to rehabilitate critical infrastructure.
evidence_quote: The Contingent Emergency Response Component (CERC) of the project in the amount of US$50 million was activated on June 15, 2024 to rehabilitate schools and administrative buildings that were destroyed during the December 2023 explosion of the fuel depot in Conakry

risk_title: Shock-Related Financing Gap and Project Replenishment Needs
risk_summary: CERC activation to address the fuel depot explosion has created a financing gap requiring additional financing to sustain project implementation and emergency response capacity.
evidence_quote: The World Bank and ANIES have also started preparing an additional financing to replenish the financing gap following the CERC activation.

risk_title: High Political and Governance Risk Rating
risk_summary: Project faces persistent high-level political and governance risks that could affect implementation stability and government commitment to social protection objectives.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance [Risk Rating]: High [at Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 07 - 24 Jun 2024]

risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: Political and governance risks remain substantial, creating uncertainty for project implementation acceleration and institutional reform efforts in Guinea's mining and environmental sectors.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance [Risk Rating] Substantial

risk_title: Delays in Institutional Capacity and Reform Implementation
risk_summary: Institutional capacity constraints are limiting the pace of key reforms needed for the project, with only 25% disbursement rate and multiple regulatory frameworks still in draft stages, suggesting governance fragility in implementing agencies.
evidence_quote: The Project has to confirm implementation acceleration (currently 25% disbursement rate) supported by technical assistance recruited to support the ongoing reforms to address key challenges including the development of the Simandou Iron ore mine.

risk_title: Stakeholder Engagement and Transparency Challenges in Mining Sector
risk_summary: Weak institutionalization of citizen consultation mechanisms and stakeholder engagement in mining governance indicates tensions between government development priorities and community participation requirements.
evidence_quote: Channels institutionalized for citizens to provide input on planning and execution of infrastructure projects (Yes/No) [Status] No [Current Date] 08-Oct-2024

risk_title: Governance Fragility in Environmental and Social Monitoring
risk_summary: Weak institutional capacity for environmental and social monitoring of mining operations, with limited citizen involvement in oversight committees, reflects governance gaps that could undermine project objectives.
evidence_quote: Annual environmental monitoring inspections of large infrastructure projects undertaken with citizen consultation (Number) [Actual Current] 37.00 [Date] 31-Dec-2023 [Target] 12.00 Dec/2026

risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Fund Transfers
risk_summary: Treasury approval delays for FNDL transfers indicate governance fragility impacting project implementation. A critical 45% of allocated funds (199 billion GNF) remain pending approval from the Directorate of Treasury and Public Accounting, blocking commune-level service delivery.
evidence_quote: An amount of 199,093,037,257 GNF (45% of the allocation) is currently being processed by the treasury for transfer to the ANAFIC.

risk_title: Cumulative Governance Disruption from 2022 Commune Account Freeze
risk_summary: The 2022 suspension of commune accounts created cascading delays in local planning cycles and budget execution. Communes remain unable to execute current allocations due to unresolved prior-year account issues, indicating persistent governance fragility affecting decentralization.
evidence_quote: communes are still executing their 2023 FNDL allocations due to the 2022 freeze of communal accounts, which disrupted the timeline for subsequent planning cycles.

risk_title: Capacity Gaps in Public Accountant Deployment Blocking PFM Implementation
risk_summary: Protracted delays in deploying 120 public accountants to communes outside Conakry indicate governance capacity constraints. This is a precondition for commune-level PFM support and directly affects accountability mechanisms for local development resources.
evidence_quote: The deployment of public accountants (PBC 3) is suffering from protracted delays. For the project's support to commune-level PFM to obtain optimal results, it requires as a precondition the nomination and deployment of 120 public accountants to communes outside of Conakry. This is the 2024 target for PBC 3, due by December 31.

risk_title: Governance Capacity Constraints in Strategic Oversight Structures
risk_summary: Strategic and technical governance committees for digitalization of communal PFM systems have not been formally established, indicating institutional fragility in coordinating development activities at national level.
evidence_quote: The activity's strategic and technical governance structures (Comité d'Orientation Stratégique et Comité Directeur) have yet to be formally established.

risk_title: Political and Governance Constraints on Project Authority Functions
risk_summary: The general director of FGPE cannot execute official documents without 'agrément' (approval) from BCRG (central bank), creating a governance bottleneck that impedes project implementation and decision-making authority.
evidence_quote: the general director does not still have have the "agrément" from BCRG, and without such agrément he cannot sign official documents

risk_title: Government Agency Pause on Critical Infrastructure Due to Site Concerns
risk_summary: The Ministry of Agriculture (MAGEL) has paused construction of the food safety laboratory and requested Bank reassessment of the selected site, citing concerns about functionality. This suggests potential governance or political sensitivities around infrastructure placement that could delay or derail implementation.
evidence_quote: the MAGEL decided to pause this activity and solicit the assistance of the Bank for a reassessment of the site selected. The MAGEL is concerned that an alternative may be required to ensure full functionality of the laboratory

risk_title: Delays in UN Agency Implementation of Value Chain Activities
risk_summary: Implementation of activities by United Nations Agencies (PAM and FAO) supporting rice, fruit, and livestock value chains is delayed, particularly those attributed to FAO. This suggests potential coordination or governance friction between international partners and government counterparts.
evidence_quote: The execution of the activities by the United Nations Agencies (PAM et FAO) to support the rice, fruit and livestock value chains is delayed, particularly those attributed to FAO

risk_title: Government Account Freezes Disrupting Local Development Planning and Execution
risk_summary: Freezing of local government accounts in 2022 continues to cascade delays in annual investment plan (PAI) elaboration and FNDL fund execution cycles, affecting project implementation timelines and local service delivery.
evidence_quote: le gel des comptes des communes en 2022, qui continue d'impacter le calendrier de planification et d'exécution des Plans Annuels d'Investissement (PAI)

risk_title: Government Dissolution Creating Bureaucratic Delays in Fund Transfers
risk_summary: Dissolution of government in February 2024 caused bureaucratic delays affecting FNDL fund replenishment and project implementation, with cascading effects on local investment execution.
evidence_quote: la dissolution du gouvernement en février 2024, qui a entraîné des retards bureaucratiques affectant le réapprovisionnement du FNDL

risk_title: Municipal Council Dissolution Disrupting Local Governance Structures
risk_summary: Dissolution of municipal councils in March 2024 has disrupted local governance structures and decision-making processes, requiring reconstitution of community engagement committees and affecting project implementation.
evidence_quote: la dissolution des conseils municipaux en mars 2024

risk_title: Suspension of Local Public Service Reform Affecting Institutional Capacity
risk_summary: Suspension of local public service reform initiated in 2023, including recruitment of Local Development Planning Service Chiefs (CSPDL), has created institutional gaps and required temporary workarounds that may not be sustainable.
evidence_quote: la suspension d'une réforme des services publics locaux initiée en 2023

risk_title: Suspension of CSPDL Recruitment Requiring Temporary Staffing Solutions
risk_summary: Recruitment of Chief Local Development Planning Officers (CSPDL) suspended since April 2024, forcing ANAFIC to implement temporary solution with 362 Community Technical Agents (ATEC) and 9,939 Community Animators, which may not provide adequate institutional sustainability.
evidence_quote: Le recrutement des Chef de Service de Planification du Développement Local (CSPDL), prévu dans le cadre de la réforme de la fonction publique locale, est suspendu depuis avril 2024

risk_title: Conflict Risk Management System Operational in Limited Geographic Coverage
risk_summary: Early Warning and Response System (SAPR) operational in only 169 of 337 communes, leaving significant geographic gaps in conflict prevention and response capacity despite 3,969 alerts identified and treated.
evidence_quote: A date le SAPR est opérationnel dans 169 CL

risk_title: Delayed Deployment of Municipal Treasurers Affecting Financial Governance
risk_summary: Nomination of 120 municipal treasurers (receveurs municipaux) delayed from August 2024 to December 2024, with only 13 appointed in Conakry without full credentials, undermining local financial management capacity and project implementation.
evidence_quote: Seuls 13 receveurs municipaux sont nommés mais sans caution et sans prestation de serment...Un retard certain est constaté

risk_title: Institutional Governance Gaps in Digitalization Project Management
risk_summary: Strategic Orientation Committee (COS), Steering Committee (CODIR), and project team for financial digitalization not yet installed as of October 2024, creating governance vacuum that previously contributed to SIM_BA implementation failure in PAGL 1.
evidence_quote: des arrêtés ministériels, en préparation, sont nécessaires pour la constitution du COS et du CODIR...Un retard est donc constaté

risk_title: Asset Security Breaches at Project Level
risk_summary: Theft of laptop computers at national project office indicates asset security vulnerabilities and potential governance weaknesses in project management.
evidence_quote: des vols d'ordinateurs portables au niveau du bureau national

risk_title: Institutional Coordination Challenges Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: The project's interministerial coordination structure has not adequately responded to the combined needs of two ministries and multiple executing agencies, resulting in non-implementation of activities previously identified as crucial. This coordination weakness is impacting overall project execution efficiency.
evidence_quote: La coordination telle que structurée n'a pas pu répondre aux besoins combinés des deux ministères et multiples agences d'exécutions démontré par la non mise en œuvre d'activités qui avaient été considérées comme étant cruciales au cours des missions précédentes d'appui à la mise en œuvre.

risk_title: Slow Procurement Processes Delaying Critical Project Activities
risk_summary: Despite improvements, procurement processes remain slow, with key technical assistance markets and geoscience works still in procurement phases. This is directly impacting the project's low disbursement rate (23.83%) and delaying implementation of strategic activities.
evidence_quote: les processus de passation des marchés restent lents malgré un engagement des départements ministériels... plusieurs activités clé sont en retard et restent encore en phase de passation des marchés. Il s'agit notamment : (i) des travaux de géoscience (levés géochimique, géophysique et cartographie) ; (ii) la mise en place d'un système d'information géologique et minière ; (iii) la mise en place d'un réseau de surveillance séismique

risk_title: Capacity Constraints in Environmental and Social Safeguards Implementation
risk_summary: Multiple environmental and social safeguard measures face implementation delays due to insufficient institutional capacity, including incomplete screening of infrastructure sites, unfinalized resettlement procedures, and gaps in environmental compliance monitoring systems.
evidence_quote: plusieurs mesures ne puissent être mises en œuvre, les activités y relatives n'étant pas encore entamées... plusieurs actions prioritaires doivent être menées pour opérationnaliser le système de gestion E&S avec (i) la formalisation du Conseil d'Administration de l'AGEE ; (ii) la formalisation des liens entre la commission nationale d'indemnisation et le Comité technique d'analyse et d'évaluation environnementale (CTAE)

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Gaps in Protected Areas Management
risk_summary: The OGPNRF faces significant operational challenges including lack of Board formalization, incomplete infrastructure, insufficient field presence, and gaps in sustainable financing mechanisms needed to operationalize protected areas management effectively.
evidence_quote: plusieurs actions prioritaires doivent être menés pour opérationnaliser pleinement la gestion des AP avec (i) la mise en place du Conseil d'Administration de l'OGPNRF ; (ii) la construction du siège de l'OGPNRF ; (iii) les constructions sur le terrain pour les conservateurs de la nature dans les aires protégées

risk_title: Inadequate Financing Mechanisms for Environmental Management Agency Operations
risk_summary: The AGEE lacks sustainable financing mechanisms to ensure frequency and independence of its monitoring missions, which are critical for environmental compliance in the context of the Simandou mining project development.
evidence_quote: la mise en place d'un mécanisme de financement durable de l'AGEE permettant d'assurer la fréquence et l'indépendance de ses missions, requises notamment dans le cadre du développement du projet minier de Simandou

risk_title: Resettlement and Compensation Framework Not Yet Operationalized
risk_summary: The national resettlement and compensation referential for populations impacted by public and private projects has not been formally adopted, and procedures for implementing Resettlement Action Plans (PAR) remain unclear, creating risks for communities affected by mining and infrastructure projects.
evidence_quote: l'opérationnalisation des modalités d'élaboration, de validation et de mise en œuvre des Plans d'Action de Réinstallation (PAR) et la clarification des procédures d'évaluation environnementale des projets de catégorie C pour éviter les conflits d'intérêts

risk_title: Governance Risks in Environmental Compliance Oversight
risk_summary: Potential conflicts of interest exist in environmental evaluation procedures for Category C projects, and the relationship between the national compensation commission and the environmental evaluation committee requires formalization to ensure independent oversight.
evidence_quote: la clarification des procédures d'évaluation environnementale des projets de catégorie C pour éviter les conflits d'intérêts ; (et viii) le renforcement des capacités de prise en charge des impacts sociaux

risk_title: Insufficient Capacity for Social Impact Management
risk_summary: Gaps exist in institutional capacity to address social impacts as required by regulatory frameworks, with needs assessments for human resources to effectively manage social impacts not yet completed.
evidence_quote: le renforcement des capacités de prise en charge des impacts sociaux, l'évaluation des besoins en ressources humaines pour une prise en charge effective des impacts sociaux, tel que prévu par le cadre règlementaire (arrêté 1595)

risk_title: Delays in Protected Areas Network Reform Implementation
risk_summary: The revision of Guinea's protected areas network, critical for biodiversity commitments and climate action, faces implementation delays with the Strategic Environmental and Social Assessment (ESES) and consultation processes not yet completed as scheduled.
evidence_quote: la feuille de route pour la révision du réseau d'aires protégées doit être mise en œuvre avec (vi) la réalisation de l'ESES, de la révision du réseau d'AP d'ici juin 2025 ; et (vii) l'organisation de la série d'ateliers nationaux et locaux de consultations et de zonage du nouveau réseau d'AP

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Constraints in Mining Revenue Collection
risk_summary: Diagnostic work on opportunities and risks related to revenue collection from the Simandou mining project is ongoing, but institutional capacity to effectively collect and manage mining revenues remains uncertain, with coordination among customs, tax, and central bank authorities still being assessed.
evidence_quote: le processus d'optimisation de la collecte des revenus du projet Simandou avec l'initiation du diagnostic relatifs aux opportunités et risques liés à cette collecte engagée avec des consultations initiales réalisées par des experts auprès des principales parties prenantes (douane, impôts, Banque centrale, BQQ, LNG et BSD)

risk_title: Government Transition Disrupting Project Implementation
risk_summary: A change of government has caused delays in launching key project activities, with municipal councils replaced by special delegations, disrupting the institutional arrangements needed for local governance support.
evidence_quote: The launch of key activities in this first year of implementation suffered delays resulting from a change of government, the replacement of municipal councils by special delegations and the slow implementation of a reform of local administration.

risk_title: Institutional Fragility in Local Administration Reform
risk_summary: Slow implementation of local administration reform is impeding project progress, indicating governance capacity constraints and institutional instability at the local level that affect project execution.
evidence_quote: The launch of key activities in this first year of implementation suffered delays resulting from a change of government, the replacement of municipal councils by special delegations and the slow implementation of a reform of local administration.

risk_title: Political Instability Affecting Overall Risk Rating
risk_summary: Political and governance risks remain rated as 'Substantial' in the current assessment, reflecting ongoing fragility in the political environment that could affect project sustainability and implementation.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance [Risk Rating] High [at Approval] High [Last Approved Rating] Substantial [Proposed Rating]

risk_title: Political instability affecting project implementation capacity
risk_summary: Project implementation has been disrupted by political events in Guinea, causing delays in critical activities such as environmental and social impact assessments for protected areas.
evidence_quote: Evaluation des impacts des activités de gestion des aires protégées : EIES relatives aux interventions dans les aires protégées ciblées a été engagée au cours de la phase de préparation mais sa réalisation a été mise en pause du fait des événements politiques.

risk_title: Governance fragility in mining sector oversight
risk_summary: The mining sector shows weak institutional capacity for environmental and social governance, with less than 20% of measures in the E&S governance framework for mining being implemented, indicating significant governance gaps.
evidence_quote: Les premières conclusions ont-été présentées au cours de la mission montrant un faible niveau de mise en œuvre des activités prévues dans le Cadre directeur pour une gouvernance E&S dans le secteur minier (moins de 20% des mesures prévues auraient été engagées) ce qui implique des manquements importants dans la gestion E&S du secteur minier

risk_title: Tension between government and development actors on mining regulation
risk_summary: Divergences exist between the Ministry of Mines and the Ministry of Infrastructure regarding control missions for mining infrastructure (rail and port), preventing effective oversight of mining operations.
evidence_quote: Les missions de contrôle des infrastructures ferroviaires et portuaires n'ont pas pu se faire en raison des divergences avec l'ANAIM. La mission a recommandé l'organisation de rencontres entre les deux départements pour rapprocher les points de vue et faciliter le déroulement des missions.

risk_title: Weak institutional coordination affecting project execution
risk_summary: Institutional coordination between project management units and executing agencies is insufficient, with weak collaboration between UGP and AGEE hampering implementation of environmental and social safeguard activities.
evidence_quote: les processus de collaboration entre UGP et agences d'exécutions doivent être renforcés notamment pour le rapportage E&S mais également pour l'appui au suivi et à la mise en œuvre de certaines activités clés du Projet

risk_title: Artisanal mining expansion creating governance challenges
risk_summary: Artisanal gold mining has expanded significantly (63 tonnes in 2022 vs 21 tonnes industrial production) with environmental and social consequences, and artisans are encroaching on mining concessions, requiring government oversight and control.
evidence_quote: Les exportations d'or artisanal en Guinée ont connu une augmentation considérable atteignant 63 tonnes en 2022, contre 21 tonnes pour la production industrielle... Cette exploitation qui constitue un moyen de subsistance pour des centaines de milliers de guinéens a des conséquences néfastes sur l'environnement et le milieu social. A cela s'ajoute l'envahissement des concessions minières par les artisans qui doit être suivi et encadré par les autorités.

risk_title: Institutional capacity gaps in environmental agency operations
risk_summary: The Guinean Environmental Assessment Agency (AGEE) lacks sufficient operational capacity and financial management resources to effectively implement environmental and social safeguard programs, limiting project implementation.
evidence_quote: les dispositifs institutionnels de gestion Environnementale & Sociale (E&S), financier et de Passation des marchés (PM) du Projet sont en place au sein de l'UGP et avec les administrations, ils ne sont pas suffisamment robustes pour répondre aux exigences du Projet et doivent être renforcés et adaptés
